methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $106M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Until is a Series A biotech & health company founded by Laura Deming and Hunter Davis that has raised $106M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a peer set of 11 companies (3 public, 8 private) in the same sector and stage cohort, including Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, Neko Health, Asimov, and New Limit.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implying a valuation of $212M based on the $106M total raised as a proxy for current post-money value. Peer-median benchmarking across 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $810M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. The public-comp anchoring method (P/S multiples across RXRX, TEM, ONT.L at 6.1x median) is not applicable due to absence of target revenue data.

The triangulated valuation range is $180M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $511M. This wide range reflects material dispersion in peer valuations and methodological divergence between stage-progression and peer-median approaches. Key uncertainties include the relevance of high-outlier peers (Xaira at $2.7B, Eikon at $3.3B) to Until's specific stage and asset maturity, and the absence of revenue visibility to calibrate against public market multiples.
